# services.garage

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.garage`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.garage.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Garage Object Storage (S3 compatible). |
| `services.garage.environmentFile` | `null or absolute path` | File containing environment variables to be passed to the Garage server. |
| `services.garage.extraEnvironment` | `attribute set of string` | Extra environment variables to pass to the Garage server. |
| `services.garage.logLevel` | `one of "error", "warn", "info", "debug", "trace"` | Garage log level, see <https://garagehq.deuxfleurs.fr/documentation/quick-start/#launching-the-garage-server> for examples. |
| `services.garage.package` | `package` | Garage package to use, needs to be set explicitly. If you are upgrading from a major version, please read NixOS and Garage release notes for upgrade instructions. |
| `services.garage.settings` | `open submodule of (TOML value)` | Garage configuration, see <https://garagehq.deuxfleurs.fr/documentation/reference-manual/configuration/> for reference. |
| `services.garage.settings.data_dir` | `absolute path or list of (attribute set)` | The directory in which Garage will store the data blocks of objects. This folder can be placed on an HDD. Since v0.9.0, Garage supports multiple data directories, refer to <https://garagehq.deuxfleurs.fr/documentation/reference-manual/configuration/#data_dir> for the exact format. |
| `services.garage.settings.metadata_dir` | `absolute path` | The metadata directory, put this on a fast disk (e.g. SSD) if possible. |
